title: MolScope: lightweight molecular structure analysis, graph export, and coarse-graining in Python
description: MolScope is a lightweight Python toolkit for static molecular structure analysis, graph export, and coarse-graining. It reads XYZ, PDB, mmCIF, and SDF files, computes geometry, contacts, and a dependency-free DSSP-style secondary structure assignment, and exposes three workflow-shaped outputs: fixed-width structural descriptor tables, atom-and-bond or residue-contact graphs (with NetworkX, PyTorch Geometric, and DGL exporters), and coarse-grained bead representations with simple connectivity. The base install requires only NumPy and matplotlib; chemistry, file-format, and graph-backend dependencies are optional extras that are pulled in only when the relevant feature is used.
